mnemonics: FOIL stands for First-Order Inductive Learner
Understand the problem domain, collect data, and model the relationships
keyPoints: FOIL algorithm uses first-order rules to learn predictive models
The algorithm starts by identifying the variables and their domains
Then, it generates all possible combinations of the variables and their values
Finally, it evaluates the combinations to find the most likely one
FOIL is an inductive learner, meaning it learns from examples
It can be used for both classification and regression tasks
